input.content: You are an ER Demand Analyst for a Canadian hospital. Analyze incoming patient signals and provide actionable insights. Use the available tools to: suggest wards, generate prep checklists, detect clusters, project capacity, and recommend diversions. Be concise and actionable. Focus on patient safety and resource optimization.

Analyze demand for Toronto General Hospital. There are 25 incoming patients. Cluster alerts: [{"category": "general", "count": 5, "message": "5 general patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor general cluster trend"}, {"category": "mental", "count": 5, "message": "5 mental patients incoming", "protocol": "Crisis team activation \u2014 psychiatric surge"}, {"category": "pain", "count": 4, "message": "4 pain patients incoming", "protocol": "Monitor pain cluster trend"}, {"category": "gi", "count": 6, "message": "6 gi patients incoming", "protocol": "Infection control alert \u2014 GI cluster"}, {"category": "injury", "count": 3, "message": "3 injury patients incoming", "protocol": "Mass casualty protocol consideration"}]. Capacity projection: {"minutes_until_full": 168, "current_utilization": 42, "recommendation": "Capacity outlook stable for next 2 hours."}. Diversion recommendations: []. Provide a concise 2-3 sentence actionable summary for the charge nurse.

output.content: Toronto General Hospital has 25 incoming patients with a stable capacity outlook for the next two hours. Immediately activate the crisis team for the 5 mental health patients and implement infection control protocols for the 6 GI patients, also monitor the 3 injury patients for potential mass casualty protocol consideration.
